pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates file at this exact path using python-docx library
verifier.py checks for output at this path, export_result.sh references this path
setup_task.sh creates document with exactly 6 bracketed case citations (lines 76, 81, 87, 92, 98, 103)

pi_items.verified_value: Hadley v. Baxendale, 9 Ex. 341, 156 Eng. Rep. 145 (Court of Exchequer 1854)
pi_items.source: Web search confirmed real English contract law case establishing foundational principle on contract damages. Wikipedia and multiple law databases verified.

pi_items.verified_value: Jacob & Youngs, Inc. v. Kent, 230 N.Y. 239, 129 N.E. 889 (1921)
pi_items.source: Web search confirmed real New York Court of Appeals case. Judge Cardozo decision on substantial performance doctrine in construction contracts.

pi_items.verified_value: Groves v. John Wunder Co., 205 Minn. 163, 286 N.W. 235 (1939)
pi_items.source: Web search confirmed real Minnesota Supreme Court case on cost of completion vs diminution in value doctrine.

pi_items.verified_value: Peevyhouse v. Garland Coal & Mining Co., 382 P.2d 109 (Okla. 1962)
pi_items.source: Web search confirmed real Oklahoma Supreme Court case. Contrasts with Groves decision, applying diminution in value standard.
Web search confirmed real Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court case on medical breach of contract involving cosmetic surgery.
Web search confirmed real New Hampshire Supreme Court case, known as the 'Hairy Hand' case, on expectation damages in medical contract.
verifier.py checks for serif fonts including 'times' and 'liberation serif'. These are standard legal document fonts.
verifier.py checks for font size >= 11.5pt (approximately 12pt). Standard for legal documents.
verifier.py checks for line_spacing == 2.0. Double spacing is standard requirement for legal memoranda.
verifier.py checks all margins (top, bottom, left, right) within 0.1 inch tolerance of 914400 EMUs (1 inch). Standard for legal documents.

privileged_info_summary: This task uses six real legal cases from contract law spanning 1854-1973: Hadley v. Baxendale (English damages principle), Jacob & Youngs v. Kent (substantial performance, 1921), Groves v. John Wunder Co. (cost of completion, 1939), Peevyhouse v. Garland Coal & Mining Co. (diminution in value, 1962), Sullivan v. O'Connor (medical breach of contract, 1973), and Hawkins v. McGee ('hairy hand' case, 1929). The source document is synthetically generated with intentional formatting errors (0.75-inch margins instead of 1.0, Calibri 10pt instead of Times/Liberation Serif 12pt, single spacing instead of double). The task requires converting six bracketed case citations into proper footnotes and applying legal brief formatting standards: 1-inch margins, 12pt serif font, double spacing, and centered bold title.
